description: Catalog of survey instruments and item banks. Skills read this index to find items by domain, language, scale type, source, validation status, AND reuse tier. Each entry points to a file under instruments/. Keep PHI and real respondent data OUT of this library. Every entry MUST record its reuse_tier and provenance so design/review know whether an item may be reused verbatim, adapted with attribution, or only consulted.

reuse_tier.A: Public domain — reuse verbatim. Store full item text. Keep provenance. (e.g., CAHPS/CG-CAHPS, ACS/Census, NHIS, BRFSS, MEPS, NHANES.) Note CAHPS rule: deleting core items forfeits the CAHPS name and comparability.
reuse_tier.B: Use with attribution; conservative about wholesale lift into the distributed product. Reference wording/scale patterns and attribute the source; do NOT redistribute the source's respondent-level datasets. (e.g., Pew Research Center question wording, GSS, academic instruments.)
reuse_tier.C: Do NOT ingest. Proprietary/licensed; consult only for coverage ideas, never copy. (e.g., Qualtrics library/QSF templates, Press Ganey, NRC Health, any vendor instrument under license.)

provenance_fields.reuse_tier: One of A | B | C (see tag_vocabulary.reuse_tier).
provenance_fields.license: Short license/terms note, e.g. 'US Government public domain', 'Pew ToU: attribution + no-endorsement', 'proprietary — do not copy'.
provenance_fields.source_url: Canonical URL the item was retrieved from (official source, not an aggregator).
provenance_fields.retrieved_date: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) the wording was pulled — instrument wording changes across waves, so date it.
provenance_fields.attribution: Exact attribution string to display when the item is used (required for tier B; good practice for tier A).
provenance_fields.verbatim_ok: Boolean. true only for tier A. If false, the stored text is a paraphrase/structure note, not the source's exact wording.

_seed_note: Seeded with CG-CAHPS 3.0 (core + Coordination of Care), ACS demographics, and CDC HRQOL-4 Healthy Days — a full member-satisfaction instrument capability (experience + demographics + health status), all tier A. RU renderings are DRAFT forward translations (verbatim_ok=false for RU) pending TRAPD reconciliation. Next optional adds: NHIS-specific health items, disenrollment_reason items.

instruments.title: CG-CAHPS 3.0 — core reporting items (adult)
instruments.attribution: CAHPS Clinician and Group Survey 3.0 Measures. Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality, Rockville, MD.
instruments.notes: EN = official AHRQ measure stems (verbatim). Full field wording + validated Spanish in the core instrument ZIP; confirm before fielding. RU = draft forward translation, not validated (verbatim_ok=false for RU).

instruments.title: CG-CAHPS 3.0 — Coordination of Care supplemental items (adult)
instruments.attribution: Supplemental Items for the CAHPS Clinician & Group Survey 3.0: Coordination of Care. Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality, Rockville, MD.
instruments.notes: EN = full verbatim field wording + scales + placement. RU = draft forward translation, not validated (verbatim_ok=false for RU).

instruments.title: ACS demographic items (person-level)
instruments.license: US Government public domain (U.S. Census Bureau; OMB race/ethnicity standards)
instruments.attribution: American Community Survey questionnaire (2026). U.S. Census Bureau.
instruments.notes: Item stems verbatim from census.gov; confirm full response-option layout (race sub-categories, education levels) against the current ACS questionnaire. Language items (LANG1-3) key for CMA LEP identification. RU = draft (verbatim_ok=false); US OMB categories not to be conceptually re-localized.

instruments.title: CDC HRQOL-4 — Healthy Days core module (health status)
instruments.attribution: CDC HRQOL-4 Healthy Days Core Module. Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.
instruments.notes: 4 items + interviewer coding (None=88/DK=77/Ref=99) + skip (both Q2&Q3=None → skip Q4). Unhealthy days = Q2+Q3 (cap 30). RU = draft forward translation, not validated (verbatim_ok=false for RU).
